question: What is the standard and maximum duration for foundation programmes at Xiamen University Malaysia?
answer: The standard duration of study for foundation programmes at XMUM is one year or three semesters. However, you are allowed to extend your study duration for up to three more semesters if you are on academic probation or need to repeat certain failed courses or wish to improve your CGPA. This means you can spend up to a maximum of six semesters to complete your programme of study.

question: What undergraduate programmes can I pursue after completing the Foundation in Science at Xiamen University Malaysia?
answer: Upon completion of the Foundation in Science programme, students can pursue undergraduate programmes in marine science, traditional Chinese medicine, energy science, information and communication technology, applied mathematics, and engineering at Xiamen University Malaysia.

question: Who is the Foundation in Science programme designed for?
answer: The Foundation in Science programme is designed for secondary school leavers who wish to embark on science-related tertiary education.

question: What are the entry requirements for the Biological Science track?
answer: For the Biological Science track, you must meet one of the following: (i) A pass in SPM/SPMV with at least a credit or Grade C in 5 subjects including Mathematics and 2 science subjects, and a pass in Bahasa Melayu and English or equivalent; or (ii) A pass in O-Level with at least a credit or Grade C in 5 subjects including Mathematics and 2 science subjects, and a pass in English or equivalent; or (iii) A pass in UEC with at least a Grade B in Mathematics and 2 science subjects, and a pass in Bahasa Melayu and English or equivalent.

question: What are the entry requirements for the Physical Science track?
answer: For the Physical Science track, you must meet one of the following: (i) A pass in SPM/SPMV with at least a credit or Grade C in 5 subjects including Mathematics and Physics, and a pass in Bahasa Melayu and English or equivalent; or (ii) A pass in O-Level with at least a credit or Grade C in 5 subjects including Mathematics and Physics, and a pass in English or equivalent; or (iii) A pass in UEC with at least a Grade B in 3 subjects including Mathematics and Physics, and a pass in Bahasa Melayu and English or equivalent.

question: What are the entry requirements for the Traditional Chinese Medicine track?
answer: For the Traditional Chinese Medicine track, you must meet one of the following: (i) A pass in SPM/SPMV with at least a credit or Grade C in 5 subjects including Mathematics and 2 science subjects, and a pass in Bahasa Melayu and English or equivalent; or (ii) A pass in O-Level with at least a credit or Grade C in 5 subjects including Mathematics and 2 science subjects, and a pass in English or equivalent; or (iii) A pass in UEC with at least a Grade B in Mathematics and 2 science subjects, and a pass in Bahasa Melayu and English or equivalent.

question: What undergraduate programmes can I pursue after completing the Foundation in Arts and Social Science at Xiamen University Malaysia?
answer: Upon completion of the Foundation in Arts and Social Science programme, students can pursue undergraduate programmes in business, accounting, finance, journalism, advertising, and Chinese studies at Xiamen University Malaysia.

question: Who is the Foundation in Arts and Social Science programme designed for?
answer: The Foundation in Arts and Social Science programme is designed for secondary school leavers who wish to pursue higher education in the fields of arts and social science.

question: What are the entry requirements for the Business Studies program?
answer: For the Business Studies program, you need: (i) A pass in Sijil Pelajaran Malaysia (SPM) / Sijil Pelajaran Malaysia Vokasional (SPMV) with at least a credit or Grade C in 5 subjects including Mathematics and English; or (ii) A pass in O-Level with at least a credit or Grade C in 5 subjects including Mathematics and English; or (iii) A pass in Unified Examination Certificate (UEC) with at least a Grade B6 in 3 subjects including Mathematics and English; or (iv) Other equivalent qualifications recognised by the Government of Malaysia.

question: What are the entry requirements for the Communication Studies program?
answer: For the Communication Studies program, you need: (i) A pass in Sijil Pelajaran Malaysia (SPM) / Sijil Pelajaran Malaysia Vokasional (SPMV) with at least a credit or Grade C in 5 subjects including English; or (ii) A pass in O-Level with at least a credit or Grade C in 5 subjects including English; or (iii) A pass in Unified Examination Certificate (UEC) with at least a Grade B6 in 3 subjects including English; or (iv) Other equivalent qualifications recognised by the Government of Malaysia.

question: What are the entry requirements for the Chinese Studies program?
answer: For the Chinese Studies program, you need: (i) A pass in Sijil Pelajaran Malaysia (SPM) / Sijil Pelajaran Malaysia Vokasional (SPMV) with at least a credit or Grade C in 5 subjects including Chinese, and a pass in English; or (ii) A pass in Unified Examination Certificate (UEC) with at least a Grade B6 in 3 subjects including Chinese, and a pass in English; or (iii) Other equivalent qualifications recognised by the Government of Malaysia.

question: What undergraduate programmes can I pursue after completing the Foundation in Information Technology?
answer: Upon completion of the Foundation in Information Technology programme, students can pursue undergraduate studies in software engineering, cyber security, artificial intelligence, data science, digital media technology, and computer science and technology at Xiamen University Malaysia.

question: Who is the Foundation in Information Technology programme designed for?
answer: The Foundation in Information Technology programme is designed for secondary school leavers who wish to embark on higher education in the field of information technology.

question: What are the entry requirements for undergraduate programs at Xiamen University Malaysia?
answer: The entry requirements include: i. Possess Sijil Pelajaran Malaysia (SPM) with at least credit in five (5) subjects including Mathematics and English; or ii. A pass in Unified Examination Certificate (UEC) with at least a Grade B in three (3) subjects including Mathematics and English; or iii. A pass in O-Level with at least Grade C in five (5) subjects including Mathematics and English; or iv. Other relevant qualifications recognised by the Malaysian Government.

question: Which qualifications are accepted for entry into Xiamen University Malaysia?
answer: Accepted qualifications include: i. Sijil Pelajaran Malaysia (SPM) with credit in five subjects including Mathematics and English; ii. Unified Examination Certificate (UEC) with Grade B in three subjects including Mathematics and English; iii. O-Level with Grade C in five subjects including Mathematics and English; iv. Other relevant qualifications recognised by the Malaysian Government.

question: What are the English language requirements for international students applying to foundation programmes?
answer:
For international students applying to foundation programmes, the English language requirements are as follows: 
1. You must have attained a grade equivalent to a credit in SPM English in your secondary education level English subject. 
2. You must have achieved a minimum score in one of the recognised English language tests: 
   a. Minimum overall band score of 5.0 in IELTS; or 
   b. Minimum score of 40 in TOEFL iBT; or 
   c. Minimum Band 3.5 in MUET; or 
   d. Other equivalent qualifications recognised by the Malaysian government. 
Note: These requirements do not apply if your medium of instruction in secondary school was entirely in English.

question: Which English language tests are accepted for international students applying to foundation programmes?
answer:
The following English language tests are accepted for international students applying to foundation programmes: 
- Minimum overall band score of 5.0 in IELTS; or 
- Minimum score of 40 in TOEFL iBT; or 
- Minimum Band 3.5 in MUET; or 
- Other equivalent qualifications recognised by the Malaysian government.

question: What are the graduation requirements for the Foundation in Science programme?
answer: To graduate from the Foundation in Science programme, you must complete six common core courses, five core courses, and four specialisation courses (based on your academic track) within three semesters. You need to pass all assigned courses with at least a Grade D, attain a CGPA of 2.00 or higher, and earn a minimum of 50 credits.

question: What are the compulsory courses for the Foundation in Science programme?
answer: The compulsory courses for the Foundation in Science programme are: FCC1071 Introduction to Academic English (4 credits), FCC1061 Computing and Digital Skills (2 credits), FCC2081 Effective Presentations (2 credits), FCC3101 Basics of Critical Thinking (3 credits), and FCC3091 Academic Reading and Writing (3 credits). These total 14 credits.

question: What specialisation courses are available in the Foundation in Science programme?
answer: The Foundation in Science programme offers three specialisation tracks: Physical Science, Biological Science, and Traditional Chinese Medicine. Each track has four courses worth 4 credits each, totaling 16 credits. For Physical Science: FIS2211 Mechanics and Waves, FIS3221 Electromagnetism, FIS3231 Thermodynamics and Modern Physics, FIS3091 Computer Science. For Biological Science: FIS2151 General Physics, FIS2131 Cell Biology, FIS3161 Introduction to Biotechnology, FIS3201 Basics of Environmental Biology. For Traditional Chinese Medicine: FIS2151 General Physics, FIS2131 Cell Biology, FIS3141 Human Physiology and Genetics, FIS3181 Chinese for Traditional Chinese Medicine (中医汉语).

question: How do I apply for leave at Xiamen University Malaysia?
answer: All leave applications must be submitted via the official e-Service system, accessible at https://eservices.xmu.edu.my.

question: What should I do if I have password-related issues with the e-Service system?
answer: For password-related issues, students may reset their password at https://id.xmu.edu.my.

question: How do I apply for sick leave using the e-Leave Service system?
answer: For all students: You must complete the online application via the e-Leave Service system, which is only accessible within the campus network. After submitting the online application, you are required to submit the original physical medical certificate (MC) to the School Office at Room B1#217. Leave applications submitted via email or verbal requests will not be processed. Failure to comply with these procedures may result in the rejection of your leave application.

question: Where do I submit my medical certificate after applying for sick leave online?
answer: For all students: After completing the online application via the e-Leave Service system, you must submit the original physical medical certificate (MC) to the School Office at Room B1#217.

question: Can I apply for leave via email or verbal request?
answer: For all students: Leave applications submitted via email or verbal requests will not be processed. You must use the e-Leave Service system, which is only accessible within the campus network.

question: How can I submit a complaint or grievance at Xiamen University Malaysia?
answer: For foundation students: You may contact the Office of the School of Foundation Studies via email at sfsac@xmu.edu.my.

question: Where is the Office of the School of Foundation Studies located?
answer: The Office of the School of Foundation Studies is located in Room B1 # 217.

question: What are the office hours for the School of Foundation Studies?
answer: The office hours for the School of Foundation Studies are Monday to Friday from 8.30 am to 5.30 pm.

question: How can I contact the School of Foundation Studies?
answer: You can visit the Office of the School of Foundation Studies in-person at Room B1 # 217 or send your enquiries via email to sfsac@xmu.edu.my.

question: What are the contact details and office hours of the Finance Office?
answer: The Finance Office is located in Room A3#711. Office hours are Monday to Friday from 9.00 am to 5.00 pm. You can contact them via email at finance@xmu.edu.my or by phone at 03-8800 6856.

question: Where is the Finance Office located?
answer: The Finance Office is located in Room A3#711.

question: What are the office hours of the Finance Office?
answer: The Finance Office is open Monday to Friday from 9.00 am to 5.00 pm.

question: What are the general IT rules and regulations at Xiamen University Malaysia?
answer: Computer and network facilities at Xiamen University Malaysia are primarily for educational use. Circumventing accounting systems or using others' computer accounts is considered attempted theft. All users must comply with the Cyberlaw Acts of Malaysia, which prohibit activities like downloading/sharing pornographic materials or unauthorized copying/alteration of copyrighted materials. Violations may lead to suspension, expulsion, or legal proceedings. Users must follow university rules when using campus computers, networks, equipment, or facilities, or face disciplinary action.

question: Where can I find more details about the IT Services Policy at Xiamen University Malaysia?
answer: You can access the IT Services Policy details by visiting the library’s website at https://linc.xmu.edu.my/.

question: How do I connect to the university's wifi network?
answer: Connect to the Wifi network named "Student-5G" or "Student". User authentication will be imposed.

question: Can I use my own wireless router on campus?
answer: If you would like to use your own wireless router, please seek approval from the IT Department. Note: The Wifi name (SSID) for personal use cannot begin with "XMU" or any other SSID names such as Staff, Guest, Student etc.

question: How do I connect to the wired/LAN network at Xiamen University Malaysia?
answer: To connect to the wired/LAN network: (i) Connect your computer to the LAN port. (ii) Go to ‘Network and Internet Setting’ and choose ‘Obtain an IP address automatically’. Wired/LAN connection is recommended for a more stable internet experience.

question: What is the recommended network connection for stable internet at Xiamen University Malaysia?
answer: Wired/LAN connection is recommended for a more stable internet experience.

question: How do I change my initial email password?
answer: To change your initial email password, sign in through https://www.office.com. Enter your email account and the initial password given to you, then enter your new password and click ‘Confirm Password’ to change. Click ‘Update Password’ and sign in again. If you require assistance, such as resetting your email password, proceed to the library (A3 Building).

question: Where can I get help with resetting my email password?
answer: For assistance with resetting your email password, proceed to the library located in the A3 Building.

question: What is a Student Pass and why do I need it?
answer: A Student Pass is a mandatory document for all international students studying in Malaysia. It includes a sticker endorsement by the Immigration Department of Malaysia and functions as a multiple-entry visa, allowing you to exit and re-enter Malaysia within a specified period. The pass details your visa type, allowable length of stay, number of permitted entries, and its validity.

question: When should I renew my Student Pass?
answer: You must submit your Student Pass renewal application to ISAO at least twelve (12) weeks before its expiration. During the processing period, you are not allowed to make any travel arrangements. Failing to renew on time risks overstaying, which is prosecutable under Malaysian law.

question: Do I need a new Student Pass when progressing to a higher academic level?
answer: Yes, if you are progressing from one academic level to the next, you must apply for a new Student Pass.

question: What visa services are available for new students at Xiamen University Malaysia?
answer: For new students, the university assists with obtaining the Student Pass and required documents.

question: How can I renew my student visa at Xiamen University Malaysia?
answer: The university provides guidance on renewing student visas before expiration.

question: What services are available for international students regarding i-Kad?
answer: The university assists with the application and renewal of the International Student Card (i-Kad) for international students.

question: What is a Single-Entry Visa (SEV) for students at Xiamen University Malaysia?
answer: The SEV is a one-time entry visa required for students from countries that need a visa to enter Malaysia. It must be obtained before arrival and is issued by the Malaysian Embassy or Consulate in the student's home country.

question: Do students from ASEAN countries need an SEV or eVISA to study at Xiamen University Malaysia?
answer: For international students from ASEAN countries (e.g., Indonesia, Thailand, Singapore), an SEV or eVISA is not required. However, they must have a VAL (Visa Approval Letter). Some nationalities may be eligible for a Visa on Arrival (VOA), but they must confirm with the university and immigration authorities.

question: What visa types are essential for international students traveling to Malaysia?
answer: The Single-Entry Visa (SEV) and eVISA (Student) are essential for international students traveling to Malaysia. Students should check their country’s eligibility and apply for the correct visa type to ensure a smooth arrival and Student Pass processing.

question: Can international students get a Visa on Arrival (VOA) for studying at Xiamen University Malaysia?
answer: Some nationalities may be eligible for a Visa on Arrival (VOA), but they must confirm with the university and immigration authorities.

question: What should I do after my SEV is approved?
answer: Once the SEV is approved, students should make travel arrangements, including flight booking. It is advisable to inform the university of the arrival date and time 7 days prior to arrival date for airport arrival arrangements.

question: When should I inform the university about my arrival details?
answer: Students should inform the university of the arrival date and time 7 days prior to arrival date for airport arrival arrangements.

question: What documents should international students carry while in Malaysia?
answer: International students should always carry a copy of their passport and student pass while in Malaysia.

question: What should international students do if they face issues with visa renewal or compliance?
answer: International students should follow university guidelines on visa renewal and compliance. If they face any issues, they can visit the International Student Office for assistance.

question: Where can international students get assistance for settling in at Xiamen University Malaysia?
answer: International students can visit the International Student Office for assistance with settling in at Xiamen University Malaysia.

question: What is an i-Kad and what is its purpose for international students?
answer: The i-Kad (International Student Card) is an identification card issued by Immigration Malaysia to international students. It serves as an alternative to carrying a passport for identification within Malaysia.

question: How do I apply for a new student visa at Xiamen University Malaysia?
answer: Submit a fresh visa application under the new university via EMGS. Provide updated passport details, offer letter, and academic transcripts.

question: What documents are required for a new student visa application?
answer: You need to provide updated passport details, offer letter, and academic transcripts for the visa application via EMGS.

question: What are the consequences of not following the visa withdrawal procedure?
answer: Failure to follow the visa withdrawal procedure may result in: Immigration Blacklisting – The student may be banned from re-entering Malaysia. Overstaying Fines and Legal Action – Overstaying a visa is a violation of Malaysian immigration laws and may lead to fines, detention, or deportation. Denial of Future Student Visas – Students who fail to cancel their visas properly may face difficulties in applying for a new student visa in Malaysia. University Disciplinary Action – Outstanding financial dues or unresolved academic matters may affect future academic applications or transcripts.

question: What services does the XMUM Counselling Centre offer?
answer: The XMUM Counselling Centre provides individual and group counselling to support students. Services include support for adaptation, self-exploration, study stress, emotion, grief and loss, sexuality/gender, family, boy-girl relationship, friendship, and career guidance. The service is free and ensures confidentiality and privacy for all students.

question: Who can use the XMUM Counselling Centre services?
answer: The XMUM Counselling Centre is available to all students at the university, irrespective of age, socioeconomic status, ethnicity, culture, religion, gender identity, and sexual orientation. The centre aims to meet the unique needs of each student who seeks counselling services.

question: Are the counselling services at XMUM free?
answer: Yes, the counselling services provided by the XMUM Counselling Centre are free for all students. The service ensures confidentiality and privacy.

question: What are the general rules for using library rooms and common learning spaces at Xiamen University Malaysia?
answer: The following rules apply to all library rooms (e.g., instruction room, mini cinema, makerspace) and common learning areas: Use the space only for study and academic purposes. Seat reservations are not allowed in common learning areas (first come, first served), while library rooms require reservations via the space booking system. Keep the space clean and tidy. Food and beverages are prohibited (except bottled plain water). Turn off lights and close the door when leaving. Avoid loud noises. Do not move, remove, or add furniture. Playing cards, games, or watching movies is prohibited. Vandalism is not allowed. The patron who booked a room must be present during the reservation.

question: Can I reserve a seat in the common learning areas of the library?
answer: No, seat reservations are not allowed in common learning areas. Access is on a first come, first served basis. However, library rooms (e.g., instruction room, mini cinema) require reservations via the space booking system.

question: What items are prohibited in library rooms and common learning spaces?
answer: Food and beverages are not allowed (except bottled plain drinking water). Playing cards, games, or watching movies is also prohibited. Vandalism of library rooms or common learning areas is strictly forbidden.

question: How can I access the internet on campus or in the student hostel?
answer: You can access the campus internet 24 hours a day when you are on campus or in the student hostel via the Ethernet/LAN or the wireless network. If you want to use the Ethernet/LAN network, you must get your own accessories such as Ethernet/LAN cables to connect your computer to the network.

question: What are the rules for using the internet on campus?
answer: As a responsible user of IT services, you are prohibited from viewing pornographic websites, downloading pornographic materials from the Internet, or engaging in online gambling. You should not attempt to remove, tamper, or interfere with any of the computers, printers, cabling, projectors, power extension cords, network devices, or other equipment in the classrooms, computer laboratories, library, or any other locations with equipment installed on campus. The IT Department reserves the right to control the availability of Ethernet sockets and to constantly monitor any abnormal Internet activities on campus.

question: How is room assignment determined for students at Xiamen University Malaysia?
answer: Room assignment is made at the sole discretion of the Accommodation Office (AO). The AO reserves the right to reassign rooms for reasons such as safety, health, repair service, economy, disciplinary issues involving residents, or unresolvable roommate incompatibility.

question: Can students change their accommodation unit at Xiamen University Malaysia?
answer: Residents are not allowed to change units without the approval of the AO. Requests for unit changes are principally discouraged, but if approved, an administration fee of RM 30 will be imposed. Downgrading occupancy type is generally not allowed unless a valid reason is provided and approved by the AO. Upgrading occupancy type is allowed, but the applicant must pay the difference in rental. All requests are subject to unit availability.

question: What is the minimum tenancy period for accommodation at Xiamen University Malaysia?
answer: The minimum tenancy period for accommodation is one semester, according to the University’s academic calendar.

question: What is the procedure for visitors to enter the hostel?
answer: All visitors must register at the Guard House and the ground level Security Desk of the respective hostel block. They must hand over their driving licence to the security guard upon entry. Visitors of the opposite gender are strictly not allowed to enter residential units at any time, except for immediate family members or guardians, who must also register and hand over their driving licence. All visitors must leave the hostel premises by 8.00 pm.

question: Are visitors allowed to stay overnight in the hostel?
answer: Residents are not allowed to invite visitors (including parents, siblings, friends, or outsiders) to stay overnight in their residential units. Failure to comply will result in the resident’s eviction from the hostel. Disciplinary action will also be taken against residents found with a member of the opposite sex inside their room at any time.

question: Can residents host parties or social gatherings in the hostel?
answer: Parties and other social gatherings in the residence are not permitted without prior consent from the Accommodation Office. Residents are personally responsible for ensuring their visitors comply with all Residences Rules and Regulations and that their behaviour is not offensive or a nuisance to other residents.

question: What are the responsibilities of residents regarding cleanliness and order in their units?
answer: Residents are personally responsible for the tidiness and cleanliness of their units at all times. Any waste should be disposed of properly at designated rubbish collection points. The last person leaving the unit must ensure all taps are turned off and all lights/electrical appliances are switched off to prevent wastage and fire hazards. Fixtures that may cause damage are prohibited, and residents cannot hammer nails, stick tapes, deface walls, or remove furniture from common areas. The University provides cleaning services for common areas only and is not responsible for the loss of personal items.

question: What should residents do to prevent fire hazards in their units?
answer: Residents must ensure all taps are turned off and all lights/electrical appliances are switched off when not in use, especially the last person leaving the unit. This prevents wastage and avoids fire hazards.

question: Is the University responsible for lost personal items in the residence?
answer: The University will not be responsible for the loss of any valuable personal items, laptops, mobile phones, or money left by residents in their rooms or common areas.

question: What are the noise restrictions in the university residences?
answer: Noise is restricted from 10.00 pm to 7.00 am on Sunday and Monday to Thursday, and from 11.00 pm to 7.00 am on Friday and Saturday. During examination periods, noise restriction is in effect at all times. Residents are prohibited from making noise in common areas (corridor, pantry, laundry area, balcony) and residences compound (within a 10-meter radius from residence building) during noise restriction times.

question: What is considered excessive noise in the residences?
answer: Excessive noise is defined as noise that can be heard outside the confines of a resident’s own unit and disturbs other residents. Audio equipment should be operated at a reasonable volume where the sound is not heard from outside the unit or a nuisance to other residents.

question: Are there specific times when noise is not allowed in common areas?
answer: Yes, residents are prohibited from making noise in common areas (corridor, pantry, laundry area, balcony) and residences compound (within a 10-meter radius from residence building) during noise restriction times, which are from 10.00 pm to 7.00 am on Sunday and Monday to Thursday, and from 11.00 pm to 7.00 am on Friday and Saturday.

question: What are the rules for cooking in the residence units?
answer: Cooking is strictly prohibited in the units. Light cooking is allowed in the pantry, but extreme care must be exercised. Residents are strictly prohibited from keeping or using gas/kerosene/flammable stoves, portable air-conditioners, home theatre systems, and large musical instruments in the units. For safety reasons, burning candles or mosquito coils without a cover is also prohibited. Residents are advised to use electric mosquito mat vaporizers.

question: What electrical appliances are allowed in the residence rooms?
answer: Only the following electrical appliances are permitted in your room: haircare equipment, clock/radio, and computer equipment. Items like mini fridges, microwaves, hot plates, induction cookers, and musical instruments are not permitted. The list provided is not exhaustive, and the Accommodation Officer reserves the right to amend it. Residents must obtain permission from the Accommodation Officer to use or install electrical appliances inside the residence area.

question: What are the consequences of violating the rules on electrical appliances?
answer: Violation of the rules on electrical appliances will lead to the items being confiscated and a fine of Ringgit Malaysia Fifty (RM 50) being imposed. Additionally, tampering with firefighting equipment, other than for emergency purposes, will result in a fine. The minimum fine for tampering with firefighting equipment is Ringgit Malaysia Three Hundred (RM 300), depending on the severity of the damage, with the final cost validated by a third party or vendor.

question: How do I report maintenance issues in my room?
answer: Residents should report any problems or damages that need rectification to the AO using the 'Maintenance Request Form'. Residents should not attempt to fix the issues themselves.

question: Can university staff enter my room for maintenance?
answer: Yes, the AO or any other authorised University personnel, maintenance staff, agent, or approved service contractor may enter the affected unit at a suitable time for a reasonable duration to conduct spot checks, inspections, repairs, or recover University-owned property.

question: What are the check-in and check-out procedures for university residences?
answer: Residents must complete check-in/check-out documents when starting or ending their stay. Units will be inspected for damages, and charges may apply. Residents must remove all personal belongings, return room keys, and vacate by 12.00 pm on the check-out date. Failure to comply may result in the room being vacated without consent. Check-in/check-out is only possible on weekdays or announced periods between 9.00 am and 5.00 pm. For different times, a formal written notice must be submitted at least 7 days in advance. Residents must clean the unit before check-out, or a RM 100 fine will be deducted from the deposit. Failure to follow procedures may result in deposit forfeiture.

question: Where can I register my vehicle as a student at Xiamen University Malaysia?
answer: You can register your vehicle at the Student Helpdesk, which is the first point of contact for students at Xiamen University Malaysia (XMUM).

question: How can I get verification for a public transport discount card as a student at Xiamen University Malaysia?
answer: The Student Helpdesk provides verification services for students who need to replace lost or damaged Campus ID cards or register for public transport discount cards.

question: Where should I go if I lost my transportation-related items on campus?
answer: The Student Helpdesk assists with lost and found items, including transportation-related items, for students at Xiamen University Malaysia (XMUM).

question: What happens if I provide false information in my vehicle pass application or transfer it to another vehicle without authorization?
answer: If you are found to have intentionally provided false information in your application for a vehicle pass or to have made an unauthorized transfer of a vehicle pass from one vehicle to another, your vehicle pass will be revoked.

question: What are the requirements to park a motor vehicle at Xiamen University Malaysia?
answer: To park a motor vehicle in any parking area of the University, it MUST be registered along with a vehicle pass permanently affixed to the right hand side of the windscreen.

question: What happens if I provide false information to obtain a vehicle pass?
answer: Obtaining a vehicle pass by providing false information will result in revocation of the vehicle pass.

question: What are the consequences of transferring my vehicle pass to another vehicle without authorization?
answer: Unauthorized transfer of a vehicle pass to another vehicle will result in revocation of the vehicle pass.

question: What are the emergency contact numbers for the fire brigade near Xiamen University Malaysia?
answer:
The fire brigade contact numbers near Xiamen University Malaysia are:
1. Sepang: 03-3142 1333
2. Kuala Lumpur International Airport (KLIA): 03-8787 4970

question: How much refund can I get if I withdraw from my programme at Xiamen University Malaysia?
answer: The refund amount depends on when your completed withdrawal form is received. If you submit the withdrawal form within the first two weeks of a new semester, you are entitled to a 50% refund of the tuition fee. If you submit the withdrawal form after the first two weeks of the new semester, no refund will be given.

question: What is the penalty for withdrawing without paying tuition fees at Xiamen University Malaysia?
answer: If you withdraw within the first two weeks of a semester without paying any tuition fees, you are liable to pay a penalty equivalent to 50% of the tuition fee for that semester. If you withdraw more than two weeks after the semester starts without paying any tuition fees, you are liable to pay a penalty equivalent to the full tuition fee for that semester.

question: What do I need to do before withdrawing from my programme at Xiamen University Malaysia?
answer: Before withdrawing, you must complete the 'Student Withdrawal Application form' available at https://eservices.xmu.edu.my/. You must also settle any outstanding fees, room rental charges, and utility bills, and return any loaned books to the library or loaned items to the responsible departments or offices.

question: What are the financial implications of applying for a study break during a semester?
answer: For foundation level students: If your study break application is received and approved before the start of a semester, you are not liable to pay any tuition fee. If approved within the first two weeks of a semester, 50% of any paid tuition fee will be treated as advanced payment for the future semester, while the other half is forfeited. If approved after the second week, all paid tuition fee for the semester is forfeited, or you may face a penalty equivalent to the full tuition fee if unpaid.

question: How does the timing of a study break application affect tuition fees?
answer: For foundation level students: Applying before the semester starts means no tuition fee is due. Applying within the first two weeks of the semester results in 50% of the paid tuition fee being treated as advanced payment, with the rest forfeited. Applying after the second week leads to forfeiture of all paid tuition fees or a penalty equal to the full tuition fee if unpaid.

question: What happens to my tuition fees if I take a study break after the semester has started?
answer: For foundation level students: If your study break is approved within the first two weeks of the semester, 50% of your paid tuition fee is treated as advanced payment for the future semester, and the other half is forfeited. If approved after the second week, all paid tuition fees are forfeited, or you may incur a penalty equal to the full tuition fee if unpaid.

question: What are the tuition fees for the Foundation in Science programme at Xiamen University Malaysia?
answer: For Malaysian students: The tuition fee for the Foundation in Science programme is RM19,000 per year. For international students: The tuition fee is RM20,000 per year. Additionally, international students must pay a security deposit of RM1,000 (refundable), an international student fee of RM2,500 per year, and the fees are exclusive of 6% SST.

question: What are the administrative fees for applying to a foundation programme at Xiamen University Malaysia?
answer: The application fee is RM100 and the registration fee is RM200 for all foundation programmes. These fees are non-refundable. For international students, additional fees include a security deposit of RM1,000 (refundable), an international student fee of RM2,500 per year, and the fees are exclusive of 6% SST.

question: What are the tuition fees for the Foundation in Arts and Social Science programme at Xiamen University Malaysia?
answer: For Malaysian students: The tuition fee for the Foundation in Arts and Social Science programme is RM17,000 per year. For international students: The tuition fee is RM18,000 per year. Additionally, international students must pay a security deposit of RM1,000 (refundable), an international student fee of RM2,500 per year, and the fees are exclusive of 6% SST.

question: What is the deadline to pay tuition fees for each semester?
answer: You are required to pay your tuition fee for each semester within seven (7) working days from the commencement of a new semester.

question: What happens if I do not pay my tuition fees on time?
answer: If you have defaulted on any of your tuition fee payments without informing the Finance Office about your reason for failing to make payments, you shall: (i) Be automatically barred from attending classes; (ii) Be denied access to university facilities; and (iii) Have your certificate of programme completion withheld at the time of your graduation until you have settled all your outstanding fees.

question: How will I be notified if I have outstanding fees?
answer: If you owe the university money, you will be sent reminders via email and/or SMS, and your name will appear in a list of students with outstanding fees which will be posted on notice boards.

question: How does the refund process work when I withdraw from my study programme?
answer: Any partially or fully paid fees will be refunded to you, less any deductible amount of money owing to the university, when you withdraw from your study programme. Your refund request will be processed within sixty (60) days upon receiving your completed programme withdrawal form and relevant documents (if applicable). All refund shall be free of interest and the University reserves the right to off-set the refundable amount against any outstanding fees or whatsoever payments due and owing to the University.

question: How long does it take to process a refund after withdrawing from a programme?
answer: Your refund request will be processed within sixty (60) days upon receiving your completed programme withdrawal form and relevant documents (if applicable).

question: Are there any deductions or conditions applied to the refund amount?
answer: Any partially or fully paid fees will be refunded to you, less any deductible amount of money owing to the university. The University reserves the right to off-set the refundable amount against any outstanding fees or whatsoever payments due and owing to the University.

question: What are the accepted modes of payment for fees at Xiamen University Malaysia?
answer: You can pay your fees in cash, with cheques, bank drafts or credit cards at the Finance Office or via direct bank-in, telegraphic transfer or online banking (ePayment).

question: Where can I pay my fees in person at Xiamen University Malaysia?
answer: You can pay your fees in person at the Finance Office using cash, cheques, bank drafts, or credit cards.

question: Can I pay my fees online at Xiamen University Malaysia?
answer: Yes, you can pay your fees via online banking (ePayment), direct bank-in, or telegraphic transfer.

question: Where can I make cash payments at Xiamen University Malaysia?
answer: Cash payments can be made at the Finance Office located in Room A3#711. You will receive an official receipt immediately after making the payment.

question: What happens after I make a cash payment at the Finance Office?
answer: Upon making a cash payment at the Finance Office, you will be issued an official receipt on the spot.

question: How can I pay by cheque or bank draft at Xiamen University Malaysia?
answer: Cheque or bank draft payments can be made at the Finance Office's cashier counter or by post. The cheque or bank draft must be issued to "XMU Jiageng Education Development Sdn Bhd" and signed and dated with the current date (post-dated cheques are not accepted). On the reverse of the cheque or bank draft, you must include your name, IC number, contact number, and purpose of payment. If you pay in person at the Finance Office (Room A3#711), you will receive an official receipt on the spot. If you send your payment by post, you must collect your official receipt from the Finance Office on your next campus visit.

question: What details are required on the back of a cheque or bank draft for payment?
answer: On the reverse of the cheque or bank draft, you must include your name, IC number, contact number, and the purpose of payment.

question: Where is the Finance Office located for cheque or bank draft payments?
answer: The Finance Office's cashier counter for cheque or bank draft payments is located in Room A3#711.

question: Where can I pay with a credit card at Xiamen University Malaysia?
answer: Payments with credit cards can be made at the Finance Office (Room A3#711) or through online banking.

question: Are there any processing fees for credit card payments?
answer: No processing fee will be imposed on the use of Visa, Mastercard, or UnionPay cards to make payments.

question: What is the procedure for making a credit card payment?
answer: You shall be required to key in your PIN to verify your identity before making any payments using a credit card. Once the transaction is approved by the issuing financial institution, you will be issued an official receipt by the Finance Office.

question: How can I make a payment via direct bank-in or telegraphic transfer to Xiamen University Malaysia?
answer: Payments via direct bank-in or telegraphic transfer should be made to XMUM's Maybank account with the following details: Account Name - XMU Jiageng Education Development Sdn Bhd, Bank Name - Malayan Banking Berhad, Account no - 5624-3250-5328, Swift Code - MBBEMYKL, Branch - KLIA 2. After making the payment, provide a hardcopy of the bank-in or TT slip to the Finance Office or send a scanned copy to finance@xmu.edu.my. Ensure to write your name, IC/passport number, contact number, and payment purpose on the reverse side of the slip or include these details in your email. An official receipt will be issued by the Finance Office upon receipt of your payment evidence.

question: What details should I include when making a payment via bank-in or telegraphic transfer?
answer: When making a payment via bank-in or telegraphic transfer, you must include your name, IC/passport number, contact number, and the payment purpose. These details should be written on the reverse side of the bank-in or TT slip or stated in your email if you are sending a scanned copy of the slip to finance@xmu.edu.my.

question: What happens after I submit my bank-in or telegraphic transfer payment evidence?
answer: After submitting your bank-in or telegraphic transfer payment evidence (either as a hardcopy to the Finance Office or as a scanned copy to finance@xmu.edu.my), the Finance Office will issue you an official receipt once they have received and processed your payment slip.
